summary: Tiny build-list repair after a merge conflict: the ftclink source object entry was restored from an incorrect bare `.o` path to the expected `.c.o` object naming in both epilogue and main object file lists.
smart_moves: Kept the fix minimal and targeted to the two object-list entries affected by the merge conflict.
Restored consistency with neighboring decompiled source object paths, which use the `.c.o` suffix rather than a bare `.o` suffix.
Applied the same correction in both the epilogue object list and the main text object list, avoiding a partial build-list fix.
decomp_lessons: When resolving merge conflicts in build manifests, verify generated object naming conventions against adjacent entries rather than only checking that paths exist.
For decompiled C sources in these makefile lists, source-backed objects are represented as `filename.c.o`; a bare `filename.o` can break or desynchronize the build.
Object-list fixes can be important even when no C or assembly code changes, because the decomp build depends on exact object path spelling.

naming_conventions.detail: Entries for decompiled C files in `src/...` use `.c.o`, e.g. `ftclink.c.o`, matching nearby `ftdrmario.c.o`, `ftdrmario_2.c.o`, and `ftemblem.c.o`.
Assembly objects remain under `asm/...` with `.s.o`, as shown by the adjacent `ftclink.s.o` entry in `obj_files.mk`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: No function-level matching tactic is evidenced; this PR only corrected build object paths.
Maintains the expected pairing pattern in `obj_files.mk`: a decompiled source object `src/.../ftclink.c.o` followed by an assembly object `asm/.../ftclink.s.o`.
follow_up_queries: Find other makefile entries under `src/` that incorrectly end in bare `.o` instead of `.c.o`.
Check whether merge conflict resolutions touched both `e_files.mk` and `obj_files.mk` consistently.
Search build manifests for source/assembly object pairs where the `src` entry does not use `.c.o`.
